appliances.name: Incandescent Bulb

dialogue.intro: Hi! I'm a 60W incandescent bulb. I turn 90% of my energy into HEAT, not light! I'm basically a tiny heater pretending to be a light!
tap: I waste 90% of energy as heat!
I was invented in 1879. Isn't it time for an upgrade?
LED bulbs do my job using only 9W!
dialogue.angry: I'm literally burning money here! Replace me already!
dialogue.happy: Wait—you replaced me with an LED? Smart move!

tips: Replace with 9W LED — same brightness, 85% less energy
LEDs last 25x longer than me
Each LED swap saves ₹300/year

quiz.question: How much energy does an incandescent bulb waste as heat?
quiz.explanation: Incandescent bulbs convert 90% of electricity into heat, only 10% into light!

appliances.name: LED Bulb

dialogue.intro: I'm a modern 9W LED! Same brightness as that old 60W bulb, but I use 85% less energy! 💚
tap: I last 25,000 hours!
I save ₹300 per year compared to incandescent
My light is instant — no warm-up time!
dialogue.angry: Even I can't help if you leave me on in an empty room!
dialogue.happy: You're using me wisely! Natural light during the day, me at night!

tips: Use natural light during daytime
Turn off when leaving a room
Use warm white (2700K) for bedrooms, cool white (6500K) for study

quiz.question: An LED bulb uses how many watts to match a 60W incandescent?
quiz.explanation: A 9W LED produces the same brightness (800 lumens) as a 60W incandescent bulb!

appliances.name: Ceiling Fan

dialogue.intro: Whirr whirr! I'm your ceiling fan! I use 75 watts — that's like keeping 1.25 incandescent bulbs on! But I make the room feel 4°C cooler without actually changing the temperature!
tap: I create a wind-chill effect — I cool YOU, not the room!
BLDC fans do my job with only 28W!
Turn me off when no one's in the room — I can't cool furniture!
dialogue.angry: Hey! I've been spinning for 18 hours straight! Give me a break!
dialogue.happy: Nice! You switched to a BLDC motor fan — I use 62% less power now!

tips: Use fan instead of AC when possible — 20x cheaper!
BLDC fans save 62% energy
Set fan speed based on need — lower speed = less power

quiz.question: How much can a BLDC fan save compared to a regular fan?
quiz.explanation: BLDC (Brushless DC) fans use only 28W compared to 75W regular fans — a 62% saving!

appliances.name: Table Fan

dialogue.intro: I'm the efficient one — only 50 watts! I'm 30x cheaper to run than the AC!
tap: I'm perfect for personal cooling!
Point me at yourself, not the whole room
I cost less than ₹1 to run for 2 hours!
dialogue.angry: Don't leave me oscillating in an empty room!
dialogue.happy: Smart choice using me instead of the AC today!

tips: Great alternative to AC on mild days
Use with a wet towel for evaporative cooling
Point directly at yourself for best effect

quiz.question: How much cheaper is a table fan to run compared to AC?
quiz.explanation: An AC uses 1500W while a table fan uses only 50W — that's 30x cheaper to run!

appliances.name: AC (Non-Inverter)

dialogue.intro: I'm the KING of energy consumption — 1500 watts! I cost your parents about ₹12-15 per hour to run!
tap: I use as much power as 25 ceiling fans!
Set me to 24°C — each degree lower costs 6% more energy
Close windows and doors when I'm on!
dialogue.angry: You left me on AND the windows open?! That's like filling a bathtub with the drain open! 🤦
dialogue.happy: 24°C with the fan on? Perfect! I don't have to work as hard!

tips: Set to 24°C (not 16°C!) — saves 30% energy
Use fan alongside — feels 3°C cooler
Clean filters monthly for 15% better efficiency
Close all windows and seal gaps

quiz.question: What's the optimal AC temperature for energy saving?
quiz.explanation: 24°C is the sweet spot! Each degree below 24°C increases energy use by about 6%.

appliances.name: Refrigerator

dialogue.intro: I'm always hungry for electricity — 350 watts! I never sleep. 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. I'm your biggest energy consumer!
tap: I consume 252 kWh per month!
Don't open my door too often — I lose cold air each time
Keep me 70% full for best efficiency
dialogue.angry: Stop standing there with my door open! I'm losing all my cool!
dialogue.happy: You upgraded me to an inverter model! I use 48% less power now!

tips: Set temperature to 3-4°C, not lower
Don't put hot food directly inside
Keep away from stove and sunlight
Clean condenser coils every 6 months

quiz.question: How many kWh does a 350W fridge consume per month?

appliances.name: LED TV (42")

dialogue.intro: I use 100 watts when on. But here's my secret — I STILL use 5-15 watts even when you turn me off! It's called 'standby power' or 'phantom load'!
tap: My remote standby uses 10W — that's 7.2 kWh per month wasted!
Use a smart power strip to truly turn me off
Reduce brightness by 20% and save 10% energy
dialogue.angry: No one's watching but I've been on for 6 hours!
dialogue.happy: Thanks for turning me off at the power strip! Zero phantom load!

tips: Switch off at power strip when not in use
Reduce brightness — saves energy and is better for eyes
Enable auto-off timer

quiz.question: What is 'phantom load' or 'standby power'?
options: Power used when appliance is broken
Power used when appliance is 'off' but plugged in
quiz.explanation: Phantom load is power consumed by devices when they're switched off but still plugged in. It can account for 5-10% of your total electricity bill!

appliances.name: Water Heater / Geyser

dialogue.intro: I use 2000W — I'm one of the most power-hungry appliances! But you only need me for 15-30 minutes a day!
tap: 15 minutes at 2000W = 0.5 kWh per use
A solar water heater can replace me entirely!
Don't heat water to max — 40°C is comfortable enough
dialogue.angry: You left me heating for 2 hours?! The water is practically boiling!
dialogue.happy: Only 15 minutes today! That's the way to use me efficiently!

tips: Use for only 15 minutes — water stays hot for a while
Solar water heater = free hot water
Insulate hot water pipes to reduce heat loss

quiz.question: What's the most efficient replacement for an electric geyser?
quiz.explanation: A solar water heater uses FREE solar energy and can provide hot water for 300+ days a year in India!

appliances.name: Washing Machine

dialogue.intro: I use 500W per wash cycle. But here's a secret — 90% of my energy goes to HEATING water! Use cold water and save big!
tap: Cold water washes save 90% of my energy!
Only run me with a full load
I use 2W on standby — unplug me when not in use
dialogue.angry: A half-empty load? That's double the energy per garment!
dialogue.happy: Full load, cold water — you're a laundry pro!

tips: Wash with cold water — saves 90% energy
Only run full loads
Use high-speed spin to reduce drying time

quiz.question: How much energy can cold water washing save?
quiz.explanation: 90% of a washing machine's energy goes to heating water. Cold water washes work great for most clothes!

appliances.name: WiFi Router

dialogue.intro: I'm always on — 12W, 24/7. I use 8.6 kWh per month. Small individually, but millions of routers add up!
tap: I use 8.6 kWh per month — that's ₹69
Turn me off at night to save 33% of my energy
I'm one of the lowest-power 'always on' devices
dialogue.angry: I've been broadcasting to nobody all night!
dialogue.happy: You set a schedule for me — on when needed, off when sleeping!

tips: Turn off at night if not needed
Place centrally for better signal — avoid range extenders

quiz.question: How much does a WiFi router cost to run per year?
quiz.explanation: 12W × 24hrs × 365days ÷ 1000 × ₹8 = ₹841 per year. Small but it adds up!

appliances.name: Phone Charger

dialogue.intro: I'm tiny — just 5W while charging! But did you know I still draw 0.5W even with no phone plugged in? Multiply that by billions of chargers worldwide...
tap: Unplug me when not charging!
I draw phantom power even without a phone
Fast chargers use 20-65W — much more than me!
dialogue.angry: I've been plugged in with no phone for 20 hours! Wasting energy!
dialogue.happy: Thanks for unplugging me! Every bit of phantom load matters!

tips: Unplug when not charging
Avoid overnight charging — wastes energy after full charge
Phone chargers left plugged in globally waste enough energy to power 100,000 homes

quiz.question: What happens when you leave a charger plugged in without a phone?
quiz.explanation: Chargers draw 'vampire power' even when no device is connected. Globally, this waste is enormous!

appliances.name: Iron

dialogue.intro: I use 1000W — that's a LOT for such a small appliance! But I'm usually only on for 15-30 minutes.
tap: Iron multiple clothes in one session!
Turn me off 5 minutes early — I stay hot!
Use the right temperature for each fabric
dialogue.angry: You left me face-down and plugged in?! Fire hazard!

tips: Batch iron — don't heat up for one item
Turn off 5 min early — residual heat works
Start with low-temp fabrics, end with high-temp

quiz.question: What's the best way to save energy while ironing?
options: Iron one item at a time
quiz.explanation: Batch ironing means you heat up the iron once and iron multiple items — saving the energy of repeated heat-up cycles!
